title: Finance Tracker: local household expense tracking inside Home Assistant
body:
Hi everyone,

I have been building **Finance Tracker**, a local-first Home Assistant custom integration for household expense tracking.

It adds a dedicated **Finance** sidebar panel where you can:

- Maintain recurring and one-time expense definitions
- Bulk import expenses from CSV/XLSX with validation and a sample file
- Prevent duplicate definitions, archive/reactivate expenses, and use guarded bulk cleanup
- Generate, review, and activate a yearly plan
- Track the current month with unpaid items above paid items
- Record full or partial payments and undo the latest payment
- Review yearly history, categories, and payment transactions
- Export CSV snapshots for expenses, current month, and history
- Choose a display currency
- Send reminders inside Home Assistant and to Companion App devices

The goal is simple: keep household finance planning inside Home Assistant without sending finance data to an external finance service. Data is stored locally in `config/finance/tracker.db`.

## Install

Install as a HACS custom repository:

```text
https://github.com/himanshutak08/financeTracker
```

Category: **Integration**

After installation:

1. Restart Home Assistant.
2. Go to **Settings → Devices & services → Add integration**.
3. Search for **Finance Tracker**.
4. Open **Finance** from the sidebar.

## First-use flow

1. Add expenses manually or use Bulk Import.
2. Open Year Setup.
3. Generate the year.
4. Review the draft.
5. Activate the year.
6. Open Current Month and start recording payments.

## Privacy and backups

Finance Tracker does not connect to a bank or external finance cloud. Keep normal Home Assistant backups of `config/finance/tracker.db`; CSV exports are also available for review and reporting.

## Feedback wanted

I would especially appreciate testing and feedback around HACS updates, CSV/XLSX imports, mobile layouts, Companion App reminders, recurrence patterns, and reporting needs.

Known limitation: this is not a bank-sync integration. It is intended for manual, local household expense planning.

GitHub: https://github.com/himanshutak08/financeTracker
